design_philosophy.core_loop: Match four or more same-colored beans to clear them from the grid; chain combos send garbage beans to opponent; prevent beans from stacking above the top of the board
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure combined with spatial puzzle-solving; managing both incoming bean placement and defensive positioning against opponent garbage beans
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rescue Beanville citizens from Dr. Robotnik by outpuzzling his henchmen in competitive bean-matching duels
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate Westernization strategy: replaced Japanese Puyo Puyo characters with Sonic franchise IP despite identical core mechanics; emphasizes competitive multiplayer fantasy over puzzle mastery; includes licensed narrative wrapper (rescue mission) grafted onto abstract puzzle game

difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based progression in Scenario Mode with 13 increasingly challenging opponents
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Scenario Mode offers preset difficulty curve against AI opponents; Exercise Mode allows practice without pressure; multiplayer mode grants equal mechanical footing for player-controlled difficulty negotiation

progression.unlock_mechanism: Scenario Mode: sequential opponent progression (13 total); each defeated opponent unlocks next challenger; narrative framing as rescue mission through dungeons
replayability_hooks: Puzzle Mode on Game Gear/Master System versions with predetermined challenge scenarios
Chain combo mastery encourages replays for score optimization

world_building_techniques: Opening premise: Dr. Robotnik kidnaps Beanville citizens and converts them to robots via 'Mean Bean-Steaming Machine'
Player character 'Has Bean' (Puyo Puyo's Carbuncle reskinned) undertakes rescue mission
Each opponent represents narrative checkpoint in freeing enslaved bean creatures
Sonic franchise setting (planet Mobius) provides cosmetic world context
Has Bean character animations reflect player performance (skilled vs. struggling play)

boss_design.philosophy: Progressive AI difficulty curve disguised as narrative henchmen encounters; no mechanical boss patterns, but increasingly sophisticated opponent AI strategies and garbage bean deployment tactics
notable_bosses: 13 sequential Robotnik henchmen in Scenario Mode represent escalating puzzle challenge

faction_indicators: Robotnik's forces (antagonist faction) kidnapping/converting bean citizens
Has Bean as sole resistor protagonist
danger_signals: Robotnik's totalitarian 'rid the planet of fun and joy' goal signals oppressive regime
Robot conversion process implies loss of agency/identity
environmental_storytelling: Dark dungeons as holding areas signal dystopian setting
Mean Bean-Steaming Machine as mechanical oppression device
Progressive rescue mission structure implies expanding liberation effort
